new_tenders.solicitation_title: Calibration of Radiation Measuring Equipment
new_tenders.gsin_description: Calibration of Radiation Measuring Equipment The Department of National Defence has a requirement for the Calibration (Alpha, Beta, Gamma and Neutron) of Radiation Measuring Equipment to support the Directorate of Combat Support Equipment Management 5 (DCSEM 5). The work will involve receiving, calibrating, certifying to the appropriate standards and returning calibrated systems; as well as working collaboratively with OEM and other contractors. This procurement is subject to the Controlled Goods Program. The Defence production Act defines Canadian Controlled Goods as certain goods listed in Canada’s Export Control List, a regulation made pursuant to the Export and Import Permits Act (EIPA). The Crown retains the rights to negotiate with suppliers on any procurement. Documents may be submitted in either official language of Canada.

new_tenders.solicitation_title: Call for Proposals (CFP) Procurement and Operationalization of…
new_tenders.gsin_description: Notice of Proposed Procurement (NPP) CALL FOR PROPOSALS (CFP) W7714-26885 REQUIREMENT : Public Works and Government Services Canada (PWGSC) is issuing a Call for Proposals (CFP) for the pilot Procurement and Operationalization of Innovation and New Technology (POINT) initiative under the Innovation for Defence Excellence and Security (IDEaS) Program, supported by the Bureau of Research, Engineering and Advanced Leadership in Innovation and Science (BOREALIS). *For additional general information on the IDEaS Program, visit: https://www.canada.ca/en/department-national-defence/programs/defence-i… _____________ The solicitation for this requirement is seeking offers for solutions to the Challenge titled “Sentinel Shield: Wide-area Detection for Early Warning Against Uncrewed Aerial Systems”. Solutions must have successfully completed Technology Readiness Level (TRL) 5 and be ready to advance to TRL 6 or higher in order to be considered. This procurement is to be carried out in two phases: • Phase 1- Build & Test (TRL 6-9) • Phase 2- Acquisition Steps to apply: Step 1: Read the Challenge Details Step 2: Read the solicitation document: See bidding details section Step 3: Propose your solution here : https://defence-innovation-portal.my.site.com/ _____________ SOLICATION PERIOD : From 2025-03-26 to 2025-06-24 2:00pm EDT Please refer to the solicitation documents for information related to offer preparation and submission instructions, evaluation procedures, basis of selection and other pertinent information. CANADIAN SUPPLIERS: This requirement is solely limited to Canadian Suppliers TESTING DEPARTMENTS: Department of National Defence (DND) / The Canadian Armed Forces (CAF) FUNDING MECHANISM: Contract ESTIMATED MAXIUMUM BUDGET The estimated maximum total combined budget available for all resulting contracts under Phase 1 - Build & Test is $10,000,000.00 (applicable taxes, travel, living and shipping excluded). The estimated maximum budget available for each individual Phase 1 - Build & Test contract is $1,000,000.00 (applicable taxes, travel, living and shipping excluded). The estimated maximum budget available for Phase 2- Acquisition is $20,000,000.00 (applicable taxes, travel, living and shipping excluded) for a sufficient number of units to cover up to 27 sites of at least 10 km radius each. This disclosure is made in good faith and does not commit Canada to contract for the total approximate budget. Should fiscal funding no longer be available, Offerors will be notified directly. The Contract Award Process will continue and proposals under contract negotiations will take priority when funding is available. Multiple contracts could result from this requirement. This estimated budget is not a contract guarantee. Conditionally qualified proposals that are matched and selected for contract award may not necessarily receive the total maximum contract value. Individual contract values will be negotiated during the Contract Award Process. QUESTIONS DURING SOLICITATION PERIOD: Frequently asked questions, as well as on-going Offeror questions will be answered and may be published in an amendment to the Notice Document. PERIOD FOR QUESTIONS All enquiries must be submitted in writing to the Contracting Authority at: TPSGC.PAIDEES-APIDEAS.PWGSC@tpsgc-pwgsc.gc.ca no later than seven business days before the Notice closing date. Enquiries received after that time may not be answered. ____________ Challenge Details: See Annex C - Challenge Statement attachment of the Call for Proposals (CFP) for further details.
new_tenders.client: Department of Public Works and Government Services (PSPC)
new_tenders.contact_name: PWGSC IDEaS Team / L'équipe IDEeS de TPSGC

new_tenders.solicitation_title: Radio Tower Remediation Work, Wood Buffalo National Park
new_tenders.gsin_description: 5P468-25-0302/A Radio Tower Remediation Work, Wood Buffalo National Park BIDS RECEIVED BY FAX AND EMAIL WILL BE ACCEPTED AS OFFICIAL. PARKS CANADA IS NOT CURRENTLY ACCEPTING BIDS VIA SAP ARIBA, YOU MUST SUBMIT YOUR BID VIA FAX OR EMAIL AS INDICATED IN THE SOLICITATION DOCUMENT. BIDS RECEIVED IN-PERSON OR BY COURIER WILL NOT BE ACCEPTED. The only acceptable email address for responses to bid solicitations is soumissionsami-bidsrpc@pc.gc.ca. Bids submitted by email directly to the Contracting Authority or to any email address other than soumissionsami-bidsrpc@pc.gc.ca will not be accepted. The only acceptable facsimile for responses to bid solicitations is 1-855-983-1808. The maximum email file size that Parks Canada is capable of receiving is 15 megabytes. The Bidder is responsible for any failure attributable to the transmission or receipt of the emailed bid due to file size. The Bidder should be cognisant of the size of the email as a whole, and not only the attachments. Please take into consideration that some attachments, when sent, may be resized during the email transfer. If the email size is too large, the Bidder should send the bid in multiple emails properly labeled with the solicitation number, project name, and indicate how many emails are included (ex. 1 of 2). Emails with links to bid documents will not be accepted. Bid documents must be sent as email attachments. REQUIREMENT Parks Canada requires a qualified contractor to remediate and repair several radio communications towers. Three towers require new antenna and repair work, along with two other towers requiring new installations and decommissions. The locations of the towers are in Wood Buffalo National Park. TWO-SUBMISSION BID This Bid shall be submitted following a “two-submission” procedure. Refer to SI03 of the Special Instructions to Bidders. CONTRACTOR SELECTION AND INDIGENOUS PARTICIPATION PLAN The Contractor should ensure provision of specific and agreed upon benefits for Indigenous People and Indigenous Businesses in the Area of the Contract. This Invitation to Tender contains bid criteria in order to provide socioeconomic benefits to Indigenous communities within the area covered by the contract. PERIOD OF CONTRACT / DELIVERY DATE Contractor must perform and complete the Work no later than March 31, 2027. ENQUIRIES All enquiries of a technical and contractual nature are to be submitted to the Contracting Authority. Enquiries regarding this solicitation must be submitted in writing and should be received no later than 5 (five) business days prior to the date set for solicitation closing to allow sufficient time to provide a response. Enquiries received after that time may result in an answer NOT being provided. INFORMATION National Parks Regulations require that all work is to be performed in accordance with the ordinances, laws, rules and regulations set out in the National Park Act. Bidders that consider themselves qualified to provide this Work and intend to submit bids on this project should obtain documents from the Government Electronic Tendering Service through the Government of Canada tendering service at http://canadabuys.canada.ca/en/tender-opportunities. Addenda, when issued, will be available from the Government of Canada tendering service. Bidders that elect to base their bids on documents obtained from other sources do so at their own risk. Late submissions will not be considered. The Crown retains the right to negotiate with suppliers on any procurement. Documents may be submitted in either official language of Canada. Parks Canada has made every effort to provide equivalent documents in both official languages, however, if you find any discrepancies between the English and French documents, please notify the contract officer listed in this opportunity as soon as possible so that we can rectify the situation.

new_tenders.solicitation_title: Steel Spares for Royal Canadian Navy
new_tenders.gsin_description: The Department of National Defence (DND) is requesting Industry feedback regarding the procurement of steel sheets, plates, and structural beams for use by the Royal Canadian Navy (RCN) and the Department of National Defence (DND). The objective of this RFI is to share the DND’s requirement and seek feedback from Industry on potential options to meet the DND’s needs. This is the first step of a procurement process and is intended to determine availability of goods over the next ten years, and the ability for Industry to deliver to the Royal Canadian Navy (RCN), third party shipyards and Canadian Armed Forces bases in Montreal, Levis, Halifax, and Esquimalt, as and when requested.

new_tenders.solicitation_title: Supply and Delivery, Three (more or less) 4WD Agricultural Tractors
new_tenders.gsin_description: REQUEST FOR QUOTATIONS GENERAL DESCRIPTION It is the intent of this specification to provide for the purchase, and delivery of three (3) (more or less) 2026/2027 model year new and unused 4WD agricultural tractors. The unit bid shall be the manufacturer's current production model that meets or exceeds the following minimum specifications. Only a standard production machine may be bid on these specifications, and a supplier will not be permitted to modify its standard production unit in order to meet these specifications. PREFERRED TECHNICAL SPECIFICATIONS The City’s requirements are outlined in Schedule A-1 – Preferred Technical Specifications. The Services include, but are not limited to the following: Supply and delivery of the vehicle as identified in the specifications; Provision of local dealer warranty service and replacement parts at no cost to the City at a local dealer preferably in the Surrey area; and Provision of post-delivery services and parts availability at a local dealer, preferably in the Surrey area.Quotations will be reviewed based upon the suitability of the Contractor’s proposed solution in relation to the Schedule A-1 – Preferred Technical Specifications. As part of their Quotation, Contractors should submit Schedule B-1 -Preferred Technical Specifications Response Form by completing the spreadsheet’s fourth right-most columns. ADDENDA If the City determines that an amendment is required to this RFQ, the City Representative will issue a written addendum by posting it on the BC Bid website at www.bcbid.gov.bc.ca and the City website at www.surrey.ca (collectively, the “Websites”), and upon posting, any addenda will form a part of this RFQ. It is the responsibility of Contractors to check the Websites for addenda. The only way this RFQ may be added to, or amended in any way, is by a formal written addendum. No other communication, whether written or oral, from any person will affect or modify the terms of this RFQ or may be relied upon by any Contractor. By delivery of a Quotation the Contractor is deemed to have received, accepted and understood the entire RFQ, including any and all addenda. All inquiries related to this RFQ should be directed in writing to purchasing@surrey.ca

new_tenders.solicitation_title: ENMAX - Grid Services Market Survey
new_tenders.gsin_description: ENMAX Power Corporation (“ENMAX”) is conducting a market survey to understand the Grid Services market in Calgary, Alberta. By participating in this survey, you'll have the opportunity to share with us your technical and business capabilities, organizational capacity, and offer insight into the types of delivery models that your company uses to deliver various grid services. ENMAX is currently seeking information about the availability of 'Grid Services' in Calgary Alberta, from organizations with: - existing deployed or enrolled DERs, - active development pipelines, or - demonstrated capability and interest in developing or aggregating assets to provide grid services in the future. For the purposes of this survey, “Grid Services” refers to third‑party, non‑regulated services provided by owners, operators, or aggregators of distributed energy resources (“DERs”) that can support distribution system needs through actions such as: - voluntary load curtailment, - scheduled charging or discharging of energy storage, - other managed operation of customer‑sited or aggregated DERs in coordination with a utility This survey is interested only in services available to support regulated distribution functions such as local reliability, capacity management, and system performance, and is distinct from AESO wholesale market products or ancillary services. This is an information‑gathering exercise only and will not result in a contract award.

new_tenders.solicitation_title: PS20261665-ENG-RFEOI Prequalification of Contractors for the Cambie…
new_tenders.gsin_description: Under this RFEOI, City of Vancouver (the “City”) requests for expression of interest (the “RFEOI”) for the Cambie Shoreline Rehabilitation (CSR) Project located along the southern shoreline of False Creek, immediately west of the Cambie Street Bridge, where the deteriorated steel sheet‑pile wall and CSO outfall are situated, in Vancouver, BC. The City is seeking responses from qualified contractors with demonstrated experience in shoreline stabilization, marine and intertidal construction, and structural rehabilitation of deteriorated sheet‑pile wall systems, in accordance with the requirements set out in the Buyer Attachment titled ‘PS20261665‑ENG‑RFEOI – Prequalification of Contractors for the Cambie Shoreline Rehabilitation (CSR) Project.’ Respondents are required to submit their expression of interest/responses in accordance with all instructions contained in this RFEOI. Term of the contract & options The RFEOI will be administered through the website (JAGGAER) that is the City’s electronic procurement portal (the “Supplier Portal”). Bids may only be submitted via the Supplier Portal in the format requested by the City. Each Proponent is solely responsible for reviewing and complying with any Supplier Portal terms and conditions which apply to and govern the use of the Supplier Portal. Further information on this procurement opportunity is available at this link All responses to this procurement opportunity must be submitted through the City of Vancouver Supplier Portal.

new_tenders.solicitation_title: One (1) Engineering Graduate – Junior
new_tenders.gsin_description: NOTICE OF PROPOSED PROCUREMENT This requirement is only open to the below list of pre-qualified suppliers under the Task and Solutions Professional Services (TSPS). TASK BASED E60ZT-18TSPS/ PROFESSIONAL SERVICES REQUIREMENT FILE NUMBER: W8485-TSPS2026-06 TIER 1 (< $3.75 M) This requirement is for the Department of National Defence (DND). This requirement is for the: - Stream 5: Technical, engineering and maintenance services stream - Category 5.3: Engineering Graduate For the services of: - One (1) Engineering Graduate – Junior UNSPSC 81100000: Professional engineering services 81000000: Engineering and Research and Technology Based Services Number of Contracts: This bid solicitation is intended to result in the award of one (1) contract for three (3) years. Request for Proposal (RFP) documents will be e-mailed directly from the DND Point of Contact (DND POC) to the Qualified Supply Arrangement Holders who are being invited to bid on this requirement. BIDDERS ARE ADVISED THAT RFP DOCUMENTS ARE NOT AVAILABLE ON THE GOVERNMENT ELECTRONIC TENDERING SYSTEM (https://canadabuys.canada.ca/en). Location of Work to be Performed: Region(s): National Capital Region (NCR) Specific Locations: 455 Boulevard de la Carrière, Gatineau, QC Security Requirement: Security Requirements Check List: SRCL Supplier Security Clearance required: FSC - Secret Security Level required (Document Safeguarding): None Estimated Level of Effort: The estimated level of effort of the contract will be for 240 days per year. Estimated timeframe of contract: 01 May 2026 to 30 April 2029. Enquiries: Enquiries regarding this RFP requirement must be submitted to the DND POC listed below. File Number: W8485-TSPS2026-06 DND POC: Bing Han E-Mail: DapServicesBidReceiving-DoaServicesReceptionDesSoumissions@forces.gc.ca Description of the Requirement: This bid solicitation is being issued to satisfy the requirement of the Department of National Defence for the provision of Task and Solutions Professional Services. The Department of National Defence (DND) requires the services of one (1) Engineering Graduate - Junior to support the Director Aerospace Equipment Program Management (DAEPM) Tactical Aviation & Simulation (TA&S). Documents may be submitted in either official language of Canada. The TSPS Method of Supply is a result of a formal competitive process which was established as a result of extensive consultations with industry. The Method of Supply provides suppliers with an on-going opportunity to become prequalified for participation in future bidding opportunities. To obtain more information about how to become a pre-qualified supplier for TSPS, please contact Public Works and Government Services Canada (PWGSC) at: spts.tsps@tpsgc-pwgsc.gc.ca. For general information on the various PWGSC Methods of Supply, or to obtain specific information on a PWGSC professional service method of supply, please visit PWGSC's Buy and Sell website at: https://buyandsell.gc.ca/for-businesses/selling-to-the-government-of-ca….
